description: A gathering for celebration, often marking seasonal or cultural events; communal joy. 'Holia' names when community celebrates—the special time of shared delight.
sound_symbolism: The word celebrates openly. 'Ho' breathes with joyful opening, celebration beginning. 'Li' lifts with rising festivity, spirits high. Then 'a' opens to shared delight.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'sila holia thilonu.' (The community prepares the festival.) Related to 'thorea' (ceremony) but emphasizes joy over ritual, and to 'pharuki' (celebrate), the act itself.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Festival as beauty—the flowering of collective creativity and joy.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Festival as rhythm—the marking of seasons and cycles with communal celebration.
